{\n130\t        self.current_line_type.is_empty()\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    /// Get the current indent transition state\n134\t    pub fn current_transition_state(&self) -> &IndentTransitionState {\n135\t        &self.current_transition_state\n136\t    }\n137\t\n138\t    /// Check if the parser is currently entering a scope\n139\t    pub fn is_entering_scope(&self) -> bool {\n140\t        self.current_transition_state.is_entering_scope()\n141\t    }\n142\t\n143\t    /// Check if the parser is currently exiting a scope\n144\t    pub fn is_exiting_scope(&self) -> bool {\n145\t        self.current_transition_state.is_exiting_scope()\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    /// Check if the parser is at the same scope level\n149\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n150\t        self.current_transition_state.is_same_level()\n151\t    }\n152\t\n153\t    /// Get all indent transitions from parsing\n154\t    ///\n155\t    /// This requires re-parsing to collect transitions. For more efficient\n156\t    /// access, use the scope stack directly during validation.\n157\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self, content: &str) -> Vec<crate::parsers::yaml::scope::IndentTransition> {\n158\t        let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n159\t\n160\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n161\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n162\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n163\t\n164\t            // Skip document markers and directives\n165\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n166\t                continue;\n167\t            }\n168\t\n169\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n170\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n171\t\n172\t            // Only track actual indent changes\n173\t            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n174\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n175\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n176\t            }\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Get transition counts by type\n183\t    ///\n184\t    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n185\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n186\t        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n187\t\n188\t        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n189\t        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n190\t        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n191\t\n192\t        (enter, exit, same)\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n196\t    ///\n197\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n198\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n199\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n200\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n201\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n202\t\n203\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n204\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n205\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n206\t\n207\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n208\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n209\t\n210\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n211\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n212\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n213\t                {\n214\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n215\t                }\n216\t                scope_stack.reset();\n217\t                continue;\n218\t            }\n219\t\n220\t            // Skip YAML directives\n221\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n222\t                continue;\n223\t            }\n224\t\n225\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n226\t\n227\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n228\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n229\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n230\t            if indent_changed {\n231\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n232\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n233\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n234\t            }\n235\t\n236\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n237\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n238\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n239\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n240\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n241\t                // Process indent transitions for empty/indent-only lines\n242\t                // Comments are treated as indent-only for scope tracking purposes\n243\t                if indent_changed && line_type.is_empty() {\n244\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n245\t                }\n246\t                continue;\n247\t            }\n248\t\n249\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n250\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n251\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n252\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n253\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n254\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n255\t                }\n256\t                continue;\n257\t            }\n258\t\n259\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n260\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n261\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n262\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n263\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n264\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n265\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n266\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n267\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n268\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n269\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n270\t                                    dup_err.message()\n271\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n272\t                            }\n273\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n274\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n275\t                            {\n276\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n277\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n278\t                            }\n279\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n280\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n281\t                                line_num_1index,\n282\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n283\t                            );\n284\t                        } else {\n285\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n286\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n287\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n288\t                            {\n289\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n290\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n291\t                            }\n292\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n293\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n294\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n295\t                                    dup_err.message()\n296\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n297\t                            }\n298\t                        }\n299\t                    } else {\n300\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n301\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n302\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n303\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n304\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n305\t                        {\n306\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n307\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n308\t                        }\n309\t                    }\n310\t                }\n311\t                Ordering::Less => {\n312\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n313\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n314\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n315\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n316\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n317\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n318\t\n319\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n320\t                    {\n321\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n322\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n323\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n324\t                    }\n325\t\n326\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n327\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n328\t                    }\n329\t\n330\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n331\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n332\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n333\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n334\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n335\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n336\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n337\t                                    dup_err.message()\n338\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n339\t                            }\n340\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n341\t                            {\n342\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n343\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n344\t                            }\n345\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n346\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n347\t                                line_num_1index,\n348\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n349\t                            );\n350\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n351\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n352\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n353\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n354\t                                    dup_err.message()\n355\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n356\t                            }\n357\t                        }\n358\t                    }\n359\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n360\t                }\n361\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n362\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n363\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n364\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n365\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n366\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n367\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n368\t                            {\n369\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n370\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n371\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n372\t                            }\n373\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n374\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n375\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n376\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n377\t                                    dup_err.message()\n378\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n379\t                            }\n380\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n381\t                            {\n382\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n383\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n384\t                            }\n385\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n386\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n387\t                                line_num_1index,\n388\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n389\t                            );\n390\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n391\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n392\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n393\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n394\t                                    dup_err.message()\n395\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n396\t                            }\n397\t                        }\n398\t                    }\n399\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n400\t                }\n401\t            }\n402\t\n403\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n404\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n405\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n406\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n407\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n408\t                    {\n409\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n410\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n411\t                    }\n412\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n413\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n414\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n415\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n416\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n417\t                            dup_err.message()\n418\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n419\t                    }\n420\t                } else {\n421\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n422\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n423\t                    {\n424\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n425\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n426\t                    }\n427\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n428\t                }\n429\t            }\n430\t        }\n431\t\n432\t        duplicate_errors\n433\t    }\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n437\t    fn default() -> Self {\n438\t        Self::new()\n439\t    }\n440\t}\n441\t\n442\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n443\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n444\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n445\t        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n446\t        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n447\t\n448\t        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n449\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n450\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n451\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n452\t\n453\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n454\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n455\t\n456\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n457\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n458\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n459\t                {\n460\t                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n461\t                }\n462\t                scope_stack.reset();\n463\t                continue;\n464\t            }\n465\t\n466\t            // Skip YAML directives\n467\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n468\t                continue;\n469\t            }\n470\t\n471\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n472\t\n473\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n474\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n475\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n476\t            if indent_changed {\n477\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n478\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n479\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n480\t            }\n481\t\n482\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n483\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n484\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n485\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n486\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n487\t                // Process indent transitions for empty/indent-only lines\n488\t                // Comments are treated as indent-only for scope tracking purposes\n489\t                if indent_changed && line_type.is_empty() {\n490\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n491\t                }\n492\t                continue;\n493\t            }\n494\t\n495\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n496\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n497\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n498\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n499\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n500\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n501\t                }\n502\t                continue;\n503\t            }\n504\t\n505\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n506\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n507\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n508\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n509\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n510\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n511\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n512\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n513\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n514\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n515\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n516\t                                    dup_err.message()\n517\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n518\t                            }\n519\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n520\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n521\t                            {\n522\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n523\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n524\t                            }\n525\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n526\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n527\t                                line_num_1index,\n528\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n529\t                            );\n530\t                        } else {\n531\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n532\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n533\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n534\t                            {\n535\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n536\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n537\t                            }\n538\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n539\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n540\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n541\t                                    dup_err.message()\n542\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n543\t                            }\n544\t                        }\n545\t                    } else {\n546\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n547\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n548\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n549\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n550\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n551\t                        {\n552\t                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n553\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n554\t                        }\n555\t                    }\n556\t                }\n557\t                Ordering::Less => {\n558\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n559\t                    // Record the indent transition with key\n560\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, true, line);\n561\t\n562\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n563\t                    {\n564\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n565\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n566\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n567\t                    }\n568\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n569\t\n570\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n571\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n572\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n573\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n574\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n575\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n576\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n577\t                                    dup_err.message()\n578\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n579\t                            }\n580\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n581\t                            {\n582\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n583\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n584\t                            }\n585\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n586\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n587\t                                line_num_1index,\n588\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n589\t                            );\n590\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n591\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n592\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n593\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n594\t                                    dup_err.message()\n595\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n596\t                            }\n597\t                        }\n598\t                    }\n599\t                }\n600\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n601\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n602\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n603\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n604\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n605\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n606\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n607\t                            {\n608\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n609\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n610\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n611\t                            }\n612\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n613\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n614\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n615\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n616\t                                    dup_err.message()\n617\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n618\t                            }\n619\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n620\t                            {\n621\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n622\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n623\t                            }\n624\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n625\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n626\t                                line_num_1index,\n627\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n628\t                            );\n629\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n630\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n631\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n632\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n633\t                                    dup_err.message()\n634\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n635\t                            }\n636\t                        }\n637\t                    }\n638\t                }\n639\t            }\n640\t\n641\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n642\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n643\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n644\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n645\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n646\t                    {\n647\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n648\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n649\t                    }\n650\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n651\t                }\n652\t\n653\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n654\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n655\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n656\t                    {\n657\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n658\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n659\t                    }\n660\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n661\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n662\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n663\t                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n664\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n665\t                            dup_err.message()\n666\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n667\t                    }\n668\t                } else {\n669\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n670\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n671\t                    {\n672\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n673\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n674\t                    }\n675\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n676\t                }\n677\t            }\n678\t        }\n679\t\n680\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n681\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n682\t            Ok(value) => {\n683\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n684\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n685\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n686\t            }\n687\t            Err(err) => {\n688\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n689\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n690\t                    err.to_string()\n691\t                } else {\n692\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n693\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n694\t                };\n695\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n696\t            }\n697\t        }\n698\t    }\n699\t\n700\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n701\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n702\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n703\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n704\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n705\t        }\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n709\t        // Read file content\n710\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n711\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n712\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n713\t        }\n714\t    }\n715\t\n716\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n717\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n718\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n719\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n720\t        } else {\n721\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n722\t        };\n723\t\n724\t        // Run syntax validation\n725\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n726\t\n727\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n728\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n729\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n730\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n731\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n732\t                result.valid = false;\n733\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n734\t            }\n735\t        }\n736\t\n737\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n738\t        if result.is_valid() {\n739\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n740\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n741\t\n742\t            // Merge errors from detector\n743\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n744\t                result.valid = false;\n745\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n746\t            }\n747\t        }\n748\t\n749\t        result\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n753\t        // Read file content\n754\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n755\t            Ok(content) => content,\n756\t            Err(err) => {\n757\t                return ValidationResult {\n758\t                    valid: false,\n759\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n760\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n761\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n762\t                    )],\n763\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n764\t                };\n765\t            }\n766\t        };\n767\t\n768\t        // Validate the content\n769\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n770\t    }\n771\t\n772\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n773\t        &self.config\n774\t    }\n775\t\n776\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n777\t    where\n778\t        Self: Sized,\n779\t    {\n780\t        Self {\n781\t            config,\n782\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n783\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n784\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n785\t        }\n786\t    }\n787\t}\n788\t\n789\t// ============================================================================\n790\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n791\t// ============================================================================\n792\t\n793\t#[cfg(test)]\n794\tmod integration_tests {\n795\t    use super::*;\n796\t\n797\t    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n798\t    #[test]\n799\t    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n800\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n801\t result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n812\t        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n813\t\n814\t        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n815\t        let value = result.unwrap();\n816\t        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n817\t        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n818\t    }\n819\t\n820\t    /// Test parse_str with deeply pool:\n836\t        max: 10\n837\t        min: 2\n838\t\"#;\n839\t\n840\t        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n841\t        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse deeply nested YAML successfully\");\n842\t    }\n843\t\n844\t    /// Test that same key in different scopes passes\n845\t    #[test]\n846\t    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_passes() {\n847\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n848\t\n849\t        // This YAML has 'host' and 'port' in different scopes\n850\t        let yaml = r#\"\n851\tservices:\n852\t  web:\n853\t    host: localhost\n854\t    port: 8080\n855\t  database:\n856\t    host: db.example.com\n857\t    port: 5432\n858\t  cache:\n859\t    host: redis.example.com\n860\t    port: 6379\n861\t\"#;\n862\t\n863\t        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n864\t        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Same key in different scopes should be allowed\");\n865\t\n866\t        let value = result.unwrap();\n867\t        let services = assert_eq!(services[\"cache\"][\"host\"], \"redis.example.com\");\n871\t    }\n872\t\n873\t    /// Test that duplicate key in same scope is detected by strict parser\n874\t    #[test]\n875\t    fn server:\n881\t    host: localhost\n882\t    host: duplicate-value\n883\t    port: 8080\n884\t\"#;\n885\t\n886\t        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n887\t        let validation_result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n888\t        assert!(!validation_result.is_valid(), \"Validation should detect duplicate keys\");\n889\t\n890\t        // Check that error message mentions duplicate key\n891\t        let error_messages: Vec<String> = validation_result.errors\n892\t            .iter()\n893\t            .map(|e| e.message.clone())\n894\t            .collect();\n895\t        assert!(error_messages.iter().any(|msg| msg.contains(\"duplicate\") || msg.contains(\"host\")));\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    /// Test parse_str with sequence items having same keys\n899\t    #[test]\n900\t    fn test_parse_str_sequence_items_same_keys() {\n901\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n902\t\n903\t     let yaml = r#\"\n1583\tkey1: value1\n1584\t  nested:\n1585\t    deep: value\n1586\tkey2: value2\n1587\t\"#;\n1588\t\n1589\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1590\t\n1591\t        // Check that all transitions are properly classified\n1592\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1593\t            let transition_type = transition.transition_type();\n1594\t\n1595\t            // Verify classification matches indent change\n1596\t            if transition.to_indent > transition.from_indent {\n1597\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope,\n1598\t                           \"Indent increase should be classified as EnterScope\");\n1599\t            } else if transition.to_indent < transition.from_indent {\n1600\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope,\n1601\t                           \"Indent decrease should be classified as ExitScope\");\n1602\t            } else {\n1603\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n1604\t                           \"No indent change should    \"Transition {} should be after previous transition\", i);\n1830\t            last_line = transition.line_number;\n1831\t        }\n1832\t\n1833\t        // Each transition should have complete                   transition.to_indent <= transition.from_indent);\n1838\t            assert!(!transition.raw_line.is_empty());\n1839\t        }\n1840\t
